build_check.description: Headless launch exits 0 without fatal errors; this 0/1 gate multiplies the rest.

requirements.description: The player marks soil and ants excavate it over time or cost; opened tunnels/chambers must alter movement and colony layout. Static layouts score 0; instant tile toggles, scripted showcases, or pure Canvas shapes/text cap at 0.5.

Forager, digger, nurse, and soldier roles are player-assigned and perform different jobs affecting food, excavation, brood, and defense. Role labels, icons, or color-only mockups without distinct behavior cap at 0.5.

Food is gathered from visible surface sources, stored, consumed over time, and starvation has colony consequences. Counters without collection routes, carried food, storage, or starvation feedback score 0.
A visible queen produces eggs/larvae, nurses affect hatching, and new ants join a food-limited population. Timer-only population growth or number-only brood state cap at 0.5.

The loop connects title, colony management, threats or seasons, queen death or survival milestone, and result navigation. Static presentation screens or pre-scripted progress without playable cause/effect cap at 0.5.
At least four ant roles have distinct silhouettes and mechanics that create workforce tradeoffs. Text names, simple recolors, or same-behavior variants cap at 0.5.
Nursery, food store, barracks, and throne room have different visuals and functional effects. Same-looking chambers or label-only designations cap at 0.5.
At least two threat systems, such as insects, floods, or winter, affect different colony systems and have counterplay. Warning text or mechanically identical events cap at 0.5.
Surface food varies by type/location and changes through depletion, days, or seasons. A food counter or decorative resource list without foraging decisions scores 0.
Tunnel depth, width, route length, or chamber placement changes speed, flood risk, defense, storage, or brood efficiency. Layout diagrams with no mechanical response score 0.
HUD clearly shows population, food, day/season, queen safety, and role distribution without overlap. Plain text/debug counters cap at 0.5 even when readable.
The cross-section distinguishes surface, soil, tunnels, chambers, depth, food, ants, and threats through spatial art. Flat grids/text-labeled cells cap at 0.5.
Digging, assignment, foraging, hatching, threats, and starvation produce visible state feedback. Number changes, text messages, or brief primitive flashes cap at 0.5.
The game has cohesive underground-colony art direction across gameplay, HUD, menus, and results. Primitive/draw_text-heavy or mismatched placeholder art scores 0.
Ants, queen, larvae, and threats use authored sprites or illustrated/pixel-art forms with readable silhouettes. Dots, letters, icons, or simple recolors score 0.
Soil, tunnels, surface, food, and chambers use textured authored environment art with depth and prop detail. Uniform fills or geometric grids score 0.
Title, HUD, controls, warnings, and results use themed UI chrome, icons, fonts, and frames. Rectangle/text or default-control UI scores 0.
Movement, digging, carrying food, hatching, attacks/floods, and transitions use deliberate animation or particles. Static scenes or single-frame swaps score 0.
